subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Cards
- **25** house cards
- **24** outpost cards
- **25** cave cards
- **9** key house cards
- **6** star house cards
- **4** starting house cards

#### Boards & Book
- **1** reputation board
- **4** player boards
- **1** encounter book

#### Villagers & Markers
- **12** starting villagers
- **18** villagers
- **6** special villagers
- **4** cubes (1 per player color)
- **1** first-player card
- **1** round marker

#### Dice & Tokens
- **7** dice
- **81** goods tokens
- **10** cider tokens
- **20** potion tokens

#### Goods Token Breakdown
- **12 each** fruit, fish, mushroom
- **10 each** pots, rope, paper
- **8** ore
- **7** amethyst

#### Coins
- **36** one-value coins
- **6** five-value coins
- **8** ten-value coins

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Center board** — Place the reputation board in the center.
2. **Round tracker** — Put the round marker on the top chamber of the 7-cave round track.
3. **Supply area** — Place coin, goods, cider, and potion tokens near the reputation board.
4. **Cider seed** — Put 1 cider token on the cider space of the reputation board.
5. **Star houses** — Place all 6 star house cards face up in a row.
6. **Key houses** — Shuffle 9 key house cards, reveal 4 face up below star houses, return the rest to the box.
7. **Villager market** — Set special villagers aside, shuffle remaining villagers face down, reveal 5 along the top of the reputation board.
8. **Cave deck** — Shuffle cave cards and place them in a face-up pile beside the board.
9. **Building markets** — Shuffle house and outpost decks separately, place face down, reveal 4 from each.
10. **Shared tools** — Place encounter book and dice within reach.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Every Player Gets
- **1 board** — Take 1 player board.
- **7 coins** — Take starting money.
- **1 house** — Take 1 starting house card.
- **3 villagers** — Take 1 of each starting villager type (setup icon 1A).
- **1 cube** — Take the cube matching your board color and place it on the torch space.

#### Villager Placement
- **Ready start** — Place starting villagers in the ready area (left grass).

--- 4 Players ---
- **Exhausted start rule** — The 2 starting villagers without hammer symbols begin in exhausted (moon area).
- **Round 1 limit** — You begin round 1 with only 1 ready villager.
- **Round 1 recovery** — Those 2 villagers move to ready at end of round 1.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
- **First player** — Choose first player and give them the first-player card.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Starting Coin Balance
--- 2 Players ---
- **Second player bonus** — Second player gains +1 coin.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Catch-up bonus** — Second and third players each gain +1 coin.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Fourth player bonus** — Fourth player gains +1 coin.

- **Round 1 start** — Begin with first player and continue clockwise until all players pass.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
- **Game length** — Play lasts 7 rounds.

#### Round Sequence
1. **Action phase** — Starting with first player and moving clockwise, each player takes 1 turn at a time.
2. **Turn choice** — On your turn, take 1 normal action or pass.
3. **Pass check** — Continue cycling until all players have passed.
4. **End-round upkeep** — Resolve recovery and income steps.
5. **Advance round** — Move toward the next round and refill/refresh as required by board upkeep.
6. **Game end check** — After round 7, go to final scoring.

- **Turn volume** — Players can take different numbers of turns in a round based on when they pass.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
- **Turn flow** — Take any number of free actions first, then exactly 1 normal action.
- **Free-action lock** — After your normal action, you cannot take additional free actions that turn.

#### Explore
- **Worker requirement** — Move at least 2 ready villagers to a newly drawn cave card.
- **Die assignment** — Roll 1 die per exploring villager and place each die on a villager.
- **Lantern gain** — Roll 1+ gives 1 lantern; roll 3+ gives 2 lanterns (max 2 per die).
- **Encounter read** — Use cave card result to find the encounter paragraph.
- **Reader rule** — Player to your left reads story text, options, and explore thresholds, but not rewards in parentheses.
- **Choice lock** — Choose one option aloud and meet one of its listed explore numbers.
- **Exert option** — Move an explorer from cave to injured to gain +1 lantern.
- **Success result** — Gain the highest reward you reached, move remaining explorers to exhausted, and keep the cave card in your cave/outpost row.
- **Failure result** — Gain no reward, move remaining explorers to exhausted, apply any failure penalty, and return cave card to bottom of cave deck.

#### Build
- **Symbol requirement** — Exhaust 1 ready villager with a hammer.
- **Purchase options** — Buy 1 available house, key house, star house, or outpost by paying coin cost.
- **Placement rule** — Houses/key/star houses go in your house row.
- **Outpost gate** — Outposts must be placed on an open cave card you already own.
- **Market refill** — If you built a house or outpost, refill that row back to 4 cards.

#### Harvest
- **Worker use** — Exhaust 1 or more ready villagers.
- **Yield rate** — Each exhausted villager harvests 1 good from one of your houses/outposts.
- **Good destination** — Keep the good, place it for sale, or put it in your advancement track.

#### Train
- **Symbol requirement** — Exhaust 1 ready villager with a quill.
- **Recruit cost** — Pay listed coins for 1 available villager from the reputation board row.
- **Entry state** — Place the trained villager in exhausted.
- **Refill timing** — Do not refill villagers immediately; refill happens in end-round upkeep.

#### Labor
- **Worker use** — Exhaust 1 or more ready villagers.
- **Coin rate** — Gain 1 coin per villager exhausted.
- **Cider race** — First player to labor each round also takes the cider token if available.

#### Free Actions
- **Buy from player** — Attempt to buy 1 listed good/cider/potion from another player on your turn.
- **Minimum offer** — Payments must be coins and at least 3 coins.
- **Seller veto** — Seller may refuse any offer.
- **List for sale** — Place 1 good, cider, or potion in your sale circle.
- **Sale limit** — You may have only 1 item for sale at a time.
- **Edit listing** — Remove or replace your listed item only on your turn.
- **Refresh market** — Pay 1 coin once per turn to refresh all 4 houses or all 4 outposts in one row.
- **Refresh restriction** — Key house and star house rows cannot be refreshed.
- **Pass** — Declare "I pass" to end your participation for the round.
- **Forced pass** — If you begin turn with no ready villagers, you must pass (after optional free actions).

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Encounter Push-Your-Luck
- **Commit first** — You commit villagers to Explore before hearing final reward details.
- **Branching choices** — Each encounter offers 2 narrative options with different thresholds.
- **Threshold ladder** — Higher lantern totals unlock better rewards.
- **Exert tradeoff** — Injure villagers now to increase lanterns now.

#### Worker State System
- **Ready area** — Villagers here can act this round.
- **Exhausted area** — Villagers here are spent and must recover.
- **Injured area** — Villagers here recover slower unless supported by potions/beds.

#### Recovery Economy
- **Beds matter** — Beds on houses/outposts determine how many villagers recover each round.
- **Potion leverage** — Potions can move villagers from injured to exhausted during round end.
- **Cider leverage** — Cider can move villagers from exhausted to ready during round end.

#### Cave-to-Outpost Link
- **Expansion gate** — You cannot build an outpost without a previously secured open cave card.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
- **End trigger** — The game ends after round 7.

#### Village Point Sources
- **Card points** — Houses, key houses, star houses, and outposts provide village points and scoring effects.
- **Advancement points** — Advancement track spaces grant village points and can increase income.
- **Other effects** — Reputation and card symbols/effects can add points as printed.

- **Winner** — Highest final village-point total wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Use official full-rule tiebreak procedure (not present in the provided excerpt).

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Secure symbols early** — Keep reliable access to both hammer (Build) and quill (Train).
- **Build one engine card fast** — Prioritize an early house/outpost that increases repeat income or repeat harvesting value.
- **Explore with a target** — Send enough villagers to hit a specific threshold, not just "as many as possible."
- **Limit early exertion** — In rounds 1-2, exert only when reward is clearly worth delayed recovery.
- **Contest first cider** — Take Labor early in a round when possible to claim cider and improve round-end recovery timing.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-exerting early** — Injuries compound and reduce your total actions in later rounds.
- **Ignoring cave requirements** — Planning outposts without open caves causes stalled build turns.
- **Training too late** — Villagers enter exhausted, so delayed training yields fewer lifetime actions before round 7.
- **Defaulting to Labor** — Labor is safe but low-ceiling; overuse leaves points and engines behind.
- **Skipping free actions** — Forgetting to refresh a row or list an item for sale can miss key purchase windows.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Risk vs health** — Decide when to exert for immediate encounter rewards versus preserving future action capacity.
- **Build vs Train timing** — Choose between immediate board-value cards and long-term worker-count growth.
- **Use vs sell goods** — Decide whether goods should fuel advancement/engine growth or be sold for short-term buying power.

metadata.notes: Guide written from the provided Above and Below rulebook excerpt. The excerpt is truncated near end-round/final-scoring pages, so tie-break wording and complete point-source enumeration should be verified against the full official rulebook text.
